title: lupinus lepidus subsp. caespitosus
intro: Lupinus lepidus subsp. caespitosus, also known as the dwarf lupine or stoloniferous lupine, is a low-growing, perennial plant species belonging to the lupine family. The medicinal properties of this plant include its high content of alkaloids, specifically lupanine and sparteine, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Lupinus lepidus subsp. caespitosus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and act as a natural diuretic. Traditionally, herbal preparations of this plant are made by drying the leaves and stems, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes.
